name: Server-side performance (RSC / Next.js)
description: HIGH: secure + fast server code. Includes: auth server actions, minimize serialization, avoid shared module state, hoist static I/O, React.cache per-request dedup, LRU cross-request caching, parallelize RSC fetching, use after() for non-blocking work.

metadata.intent: Optimize Next.js server code (RSC, server actions, route handlers) for performance and correctness

must_not: Store request/user data in mutable module scope
Duplicate serialization of derived arrays/objects in RSC props
conventions: Prefer module-level hoisting for static I/O; use caches with clear scoping (per-request vs cross-request).

steps.description: Treat Server Actions like public API endpoints: validate input, authenticate, authorize inside the action.
At RSC→client boundaries, pass only fields the client uses. Avoid shipping whole objects/arrays when only 1–2 fields are rendered.
Do not store request/user data in mutable module variables (race conditions + data leaks). Keep request data in render tree scope.
Hoist static I/O (fonts/logos/config/templates) to module level so it runs once per module load, not per request.
Use `React.cache()` to deduplicate per-request work (auth/db/heavy compute). Avoid cache misses from inline object args.
Use an LRU for cross-request caching when sequential user actions reuse data. Be explicit about TTL/max and invalidation strategy.
Parallelize RSC data fetching by splitting into sibling async components or composing layout so fetches start concurrently.
Use `after()` for logging/analytics/cache invalidation that should not block the response.
